overall_justification: The Correio da Manhã edition of 1906-09-01 contains a highly critical article on Brazil's relationship with London bankers, specifically Rothschild, in light of their disapproval of the Convênio de Taubaté. The article explicitly frames the bankers' intervention as an affront to Brazil's sovereignty and probity, echoing key Orthodox concerns. While acknowledging Rothschild's interest in Brazil's financial health, the article strongly condemns their attempts to influence internal policy. It champions national pride and fiscal responsibility as paramount, aligning perfectly with the core tenets of the Orthodox perspective on the "Caixa de Conversão" debate. The article's placement and length further emphasize its importance and the newspaper's stance.

supporting_evidence.quote: No Brasil, acima dos poderes publicos, acima mesmo do próprio povo, ha uma outra soberania, mais alta e elevada, a que as leis não se reportam, mas que de facto e a todo momento se afirma, se impõe e domina. E' a soberania dos banqueiros, são os grandes senhores Rothschild.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This opening statement sets the critical tone and identifies Rothschild as a key actor undermining Brazil's sovereignty, a core concern for Orthodox advocates.

supporting_evidence.quote: Mas insurgirem-se contra uma lei do paiz, censurarem uma deliberação publica, levarem a ousadia ao ponto de quererem obstar a execução de uma medida legal, é uma injuria, é uma affronta, uma audacia desmarcada que um povo independente nunca soffreria.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote directly criticizes Rothschild's interference in Brazilian policy, emphasizing the insult to national pride and legal processes, aligning with Orthodox anxieties about external influence.

supporting_evidence.quote: Emquanto o Brasil não mentir á sua palavra e pagar com pontualidade os juros de suas dividas, poderá repellir com altivez estas interferencias insólitas dos credores na sua politica interna, sem perder e diminuir na estima e sympathia dos mesmos.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This reinforces the Orthodox emphasis on fiscal responsibility ("probidade do paiz") as the key to maintaining national dignity and resisting external pressures, implicitly criticizing the Convênio's potential financial risks.
